title: Nasdaq and S&P 500 slip while Dow hits record close
full_text_summary: Dow closed at second straight record high (51,999.67, +0.64%). S&P 500 fell 0.57% to 7,511.35. Nasdaq dropped 1.15% to 26,376.34. Technology sector worst performer (-2.3%), financials led (+1.5%). Semiconductors (SOX) crushed -5.7%. Oil plunged 5.8% on US-Iran interim deal details. SpaceX closed $201.80 (+4.8%), market cap surpassed Amazon, briefly exceeded Microsoft intraday, now 5th-most valuable US company. NYSE advancers led decliners 1.06-to-1. Nasdaq decliners led 1.44-to-1. Total volume 20.98B shares. Fed meeting begins Wednesday; rates expected unchanged at 3.50-3.75%.
summary: Nasdaq and S&P 500 slip while Dow hits record close

title: June Fed Meeting: Live Updates and Commentary
full_text_summary: First FOMC meeting under new Chair Kevin Warsh (confirmed 54-45 May 13). Rates expected unchanged at 3.50-3.75%. Warsh is not expected to submit a dot to the SEP — unprecedented. CPI headline 4.2% YoY (highest since April 2023). CME FedWatch shows 97% chance of no change. Hilsenrath survey: 17 of 32 former Fed officials say rate hike likely appropriate in 2026. Median year-end PCE forecast 3.5% vs 2% target. Warsh historically hawkish but recently advocated for lower rates. Skeptical of forward guidance, dot plots, press conferences. Analysts watching: inflation framing, forward guidance stance, communication style, Fed independence defense, balance sheet focus.
summary: June Fed Meeting: Live Updates and Commentary

title: Stock market today: Live updates - June 16, 2026
full_text_summary: S&P 500 futures +0.1%, Nasdaq-100 futures +0.6%, Dow futures marginally lower. Chip stocks bouncing in premarket: ASML +4%, Intel +3%, SOXQ +2.3%. SpaceX +3.5% premarket (shares up ~50% from $135 IPO). Asia mostly higher: Nikkei +0.72% new record at 69,902.25; Kospi +1.58%; CSI 300 +0.97%; Hang Seng -0.74%. Japan May exports +17% YoY (fastest since Nov 2022), semiconductors +61.2%. BoJ raised policy rate 25bps to 1% (highest in over 30 years). Europe: Stoxx 600 +0.3%, DAX -0.38% (BMW -7.5% on profit warning citing weak China demand and war disruptions). Earnings: KMX and JBL before open Wednesday; ACN and KR Thursday.

title: Oil extends fall, stocks steady as traders wait on Warsh
full_text_summary: Brent crude dived below $80, down more than one-third from recent peaks. US-Iran deal to be signed Friday; Iran's exports could add ~2% of global demand (Westpac). Strait of Hormuz blockaded for 3 months; US strategic oil reserves at lowest since 1983. Wall Street futures slightly higher in Asia. European futures slipped 0.2%. Nikkei hit record high. TSMC fell 1.7% dragging Taiwan 1% lower. DXY steady at 99.50. Gold bounced from $4,000 support to $4,300 (still down >20% from January). Bitcoin near $65,900. Japan 10Y yield -4bps to 2.61%; Australia 10Y -6bps to 4.78%. Warsh must balance dovish president (Trump) with hawkish market pricing.
summary: Oil extends fall, stocks steady as traders wait on Warsh

title: US Dollar Technical Outlook: DXY at Crucial Level as Traders Brace for Fed
full_text_summary: DXY at 99.51, testing major uptrend support at 99.50. 52-week range: 95.55-100.64. Break or hold at 99.50 will define next directional move. FOMC decision and updated dot plot are key catalysts. USD/JPY rally stalling at intervention zone near 160.
summary: US Dollar Technical Outlook: DXY at Crucial Level as Traders Brace for Fed

title: US 10 Year Treasury Note Yield - Quote - Chart - Historical Data
full_text_summary: 10Y Treasury yield eased to 4.44% on June 17 from 4.45% previous session. Down ~16bps over past month. 2Y at 4.06%, 30Y at 4.93%, 10Y TIPS at 2.13% (breakeven 2.31%). December rate hike probability fell to ~56% as oil decline eased inflation fears. CPI headline 4.2%, core 2.9%. Fed funds rate at 3.75%. Short-end yields falling significantly year-over-year; curve steepening from long end. Upcoming data: retail sales, pending home sales, housing starts.

title: CarMax, Jabil And 3 Stocks To Watch Heading Into Wednesday
full_text_summary: Earnings today: CarMax (KMX) expected EPS $0.94 on $7.41B revenue, shares -0.9% after hours to $51.66. Jabil (JBL) expected EPS $3.10 on $8.61B revenue, shares +0.8% after hours to $378.51. La-Z-Boy (LZB) reported Q4 beat: EPS $1.26 vs $0.82 est, revenue $570.34M vs $569.23M est, announced $300M buyback, shares +17.7% to $41.25. Sophia Genetics (SOPH) -4.9% after announcing proposed public offering.
summary: CarMax, Jabil And 3 Stocks To Watch Heading Into Wednesday
